topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. A single, curt sentence: "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise party is cancelled." I stared at the screen, my heart hammering against my ribs with a frantic, uneven rhythm. For a full minute, nothing happened. Then, a slow, hot wave washed over me, starting in my chest and spreading outward until my hands were shaking uncontrollably. I snatched up my phone, dialing Sarah, my voice tight and dangerously low. "Cancelled? Are you serious?" Her reply, when it came, was clipped and apologetic, which only fueled the simmering heat rising in my throat. I slammed the phone down onto the desk, the plastic protesting with a sharp crack. I stood up, pacing the length of the living room, kicking at a loose floorboard. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to snap. This wasn't just disappointing; this felt like a deliberate slight, a profound disrespect for the effort everyone had poured into this secret.

[story 2]

I reread the message three times. *Cancelled*. The word echoed in the silence of my apartment, amplifying into a deafening roar inside my skull. My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I walked over to the window, pulling the curtain back just enough to peer out at the indifferent streetlights. A deep, frustrated growl rumbled in my chest, a sound I barely recognized. I spun around, my eyes blazing with a fierce, uncontainable intensity. "Unforeseen circumstances?" I spat the words out, the tone sharp enough to cut glass. I grabbed the nearest heavy object—a ceramic vase—and hurled it against the wall. The sound shattered the quiet, and the resulting spray of dust made me breathe in ragged, shallow gasps, my knuckles white where I gripped the air. I needed answers, and right now, the only thing I felt was a burning need to confront whoever had made this decision.

[story 3]

When the notification finally registered, the world seemed to tilt. My breath hitched, a sharp, involuntary gasp that sounded far too loud in the quiet room. I didn't move for a long moment, just standing there, rooted to the spot, feeling a profound, vibrating tension spread through my limbs. Slowly, deliberately, I typed a reply, my fingers moving with a jerky, almost violent speed. The words that emerged were clipped, devoid of warmth, each one laced with pure, unadulterated displeasure. I watched the little ‘sent’ arrow move across the screen, a small act of defiance against the crushing reality of the situation. A tight knot formed in my stomach, tightening with every passing second. I wanted to scream, to shatter something tangible, but I held myself still, channeling the building pressure into a rigid stillness, a silent promise of retribution hanging heavy in the air.
